title: STAR Incident - Enigma Hallucination Control in RAG Outputs
content:
Incident: Hallucination risk in production document extraction

Situation:
- Production RAG workflows generated occasional plausible but incorrect extracted values.
- In compliance-oriented contexts, low-frequency factual errors still created high operational risk.

Task:
- Reduce hallucination-related extraction errors while keeping throughput and latency within operational targets.

Action:
- Added structured output validation layers and post-extraction consistency checks.
- Introduced confidence-aware routing and fallback handling for low-certainty outputs.
- Tightened retrieval quality with chunking and metadata filter tuning to improve evidence relevance.
- Monitored quality signals and iterated on retrieval-plus-validation behavior in production.

Result:
- Source-guided narrative indicates improved factual reliability and stronger trust in automated outputs.
- Established repeatable guardrail patterns for future RAG features.
